Requirement: Uses 4 connection nodes -- Terminal1, Terminal2, MCU1, MCU2
Uses 3 serial links: PC1<->MCU1, MCU1<->MCU2, MCU2<->PC2
Must use different baud rates for each serial link
Must use different UART modules for each MCU
Cannot use baud rates 115200, 38400, 57600
Display Main Menu on PC Terminal 1 (PC1)
Display Waiting Screen on PC Terminal 2 (PC2)
System stays in selected communication mode until it exits
Use UART RX interrupt to read user input from Main Menu (1,2,3)
Input '1' and hit enter on PC1 in Main Menu to enter Mode 1
Mode 1: PC2 only displays waiting screen
Mode 1: PC1 shows Mode 1 Menu
Mode 1: Use UART RX interrupt to read user input from Mode 1 Menu (1,2,3)
Mode 1: Input '1' and hit enter on PC1 to select 'Change LED color' option
Mode 1: Display user prompt to input a color to PC1 to change LED color
Mode 1: Use UART RX interrupt to read user input (d,r,g,b,y,c,m,w)
Mode 1: Display on PC1 line 1 the active LED color
Mode 1: MCU1 LED displays user LED selection
Mode 1: Displays Mode 1 Menu again after completing Option 1
Mode 1: Input 2 and hit enter on PC1 to select 'Change LED brightness' option
Mode 1: Display user prompt to input number from 0 to 100 on PC1
Mode 1: Use UART RX interrupt to read user input (0-100)
Mode 1: Use PWM duty cycle to set LED brightness based on user input
Mode 1: Display on PC1 line 1 the current LED brightness
Mode 1: Displays Mode 1 Menu again after completing Option 2
Mode 1: Enter '3' and hit enter on PC1 to exit Mode 1 Menu
Mode 1: Displays Main Menu on PC1 after exiting Mode 1
Enter '2' and hit enter on PC1 in Main Menu to enter Mode 2
Mode 2: MCU1 starts in transmit mode
Mode 2: MCU2 starts in waiting mode
Mode 2: PC1 always displays MCU number + exit instructions
Mode 2: PC2 always displays MCU number
Mode 2: PC1 begins by displaying color wheel instructions
Mode 2: PC2 begins by displaying wait screen
Mode 2: PC1 begins by displaying current color
Mode 2: Use Port F interrupt on SW1 and SW2
Mode 2: SW2 changes LED on transmission MCU
Mode 2: SW2 updates PC1 display current color
Mode 2: SW2 updates transmission MCU LED to match current color
Mode 2: SW1 on transmission MCU sends color to waiting MCU
Mode 2: Use UART RX interrupt on waiting MCU to receive color
Mode 2: Waiting MCU LED color updates to received color
Mode 2: SW1 changes transmission MCU to waiting MCU
Mode 2: SW1 changes waiting MCU to transmission MCU
Mode 2: Updates waiting MCU PC display to show waiting screen and current color
Mode 2: Updates transmission MCU PC display to show color wheel instructions and current color
Mode 2: Input '^' and hit enter on PC1 to exit mode at any time
Mode 2: Use UART0 RX Interrupt to capture mode termination
Mode 2: Both MCU LEDs turn off when mode is terminated
Mode 2: Display Main Menu on PC1 after mode termination
Mode 2: Display Waiting Screen on PC2 after mode termination
Enter '3' and hit enter on PC1 in Main Menu to enter Mode 3
Mode 3: MCU1 begins in sending mode
Mode 3: MCU2 begins in receiving mode
Mode 3: PC1 displays MCU number, Chat Room title, and exit instructions at all times
Mode 3: PC2 displays MCU number, Chat Room title, and exit instructions at all times
Mode 3: PC1 prompts user to type a message and hit enter to send
Mode 3: Message must have less than 30 characters
Mode 3: Use UART RX interrupt on receiving MCU to read sent message
Mode 3: After sending message, update receiving MCU PC to display the sent message on a new line
Mode 3: After sending message, sending MCU becomes receiving MCU
Mode 3: After sending message, receiving MCU becomes sending MCU
Mode 3: Both PC terminals display full message history
Mode 3: Use Port F interrupt on SW1
Mode 3: Press SW1 on either MCU to end mode
Mode 3: Display Main Menu on PC1 after Mode 3 termination
Mode 3: Display Waiting Screen on PC2 after Mode 3 termination
Onboard Switches set corresponding pin to high when pressed
Onboard Switches trigger corresponding interrupt
All onboard LED combinations can be shown

UART7 RX interrupt successfully receives transmission
UART0 RX interrupt successfully transmits data
UART5 RX interrupt successfully transmits data
UART7 RX interrupt successfully transmits data
